clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
Back pain
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Whole Brain Radiation
External beam radiation to the thoracic spine

History of Present Illness:
___ transferred from ___ with LBP and radicular 
pain to his right leg. The patient states this started ___ 
months ago but has gotten worse over the last month. He was 
being evaluated in the chronic pain clinic on ___ and had an 
epidural injection and was complaining of 10 out of 10 pain 
afterwards and was therefore admitted. The pain goes down the 
anterior and posterior aspects of his leg. He has received 1 
epidural injection for this pain. He also notes pain in his RUE, 
however this is not nearly as bothersome as his RLE pain.  

He reports no saddle anesthesia or bowel/bladder changes. He has 
no numbness tingling other than numbness in his right thumb that 
has been present for many years. He has not chest pain, sob, 
n/v.

Past Medical History:
Glaucoma
OA

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
Uncle died of prostate cancer at approx. age ___. No other family 
history of malignancy. No family history of heart disease, 
diabetes. No significant family history.

Hospital Course:
Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male who presented with back pain, 
fatigue, and significant weight loss, subsequently found to have 
multiple lesions of the spine, skull, and brain concerning for 
metastatic malignancy.

# Metastatic malignancy, unknown primary: On this admission, 
patient presented with significant radicular back pain in the 
setting of weight loss and continued fatigue. Patient was assess 
for his back pain with imaging, which was notable for metastatic 
disease burden, with multiple soft tissue and bony lesions 
involving the spine, liver, brain, lung and multiple bone 
locations identified. Patient underwent an uncomplicated Right 
pelvic biopsy on ___, and tissue was sent for pathological 
analysis. Initial pathology was notable for malignant cells, 
likely adenocarcinoma. Final pathology was notable for carcinoma 
strongly positive for CK7, positive for CDX2, and negative for 
CK20, PAX8, and TTF-1, which although not specific, was 
suggestive of a pancreaticobiliary,  upper GI or colorectal 
origin. An EGD was performed on this admission which was was 
unremarkable for a notable GI mass. PET CT was notable for 
extensive FDG avid metastatic disease throughout the bilateral 
lungs, mediastinum, right hilum, liver, left gluteal 
musculature, and osseous structures, with nodefinitive primary 
malignancy identified by PET. However, based on patients 
distribution of disease with significant pulmonary disease 
burden including a 4.2cm right suprahilar mass obstr
[... truncated ...]

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from note):
PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS:
Metastatic adenocarcinoma of unknown primary
Vertebral pathologic fracture
Back pain

SECONDARY DIAGNOSIS:
Superior vena cava syndrome
Parotitis

reports_summary.excerpt:

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic adenocarcinoma of uncertain
primary with diffuse lung and spine metastasis, demonstrated to have new R
hydroureteronephrosis on MRI. // Please eval new R hydroureteronephrosis

TECHNIQUE:  Single phase split bolus contrast: MDCT axial images were acquired
through the abdomen and pelvis follo

EXAMINATION:  MRI THORACIC AND LUMBAR PT6 MR SPINE

INDICATION:  History of metastatic cancer, likely lung adenocarcinoma with new
finding of T1 vertebral body lesion with possible erosion into the spinal
canal.

TECHNIQUE:  Sagittal imaging was performed with T2, T1, and STIR technique.
